CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST
Memorial Regional Medical Center — Department of Radiology
Study date: 2025-09-08
Ordering provider: R. Patel, MD (Pulmonology)
Indication: Chronic cough, weight loss, abnormal chest x-ray.

TECHNIQUE
Helical CT of the chest was performed following intravenous administration
of 100 mL of iohexol. Images were reconstructed at 1.25 mm and 5 mm slice
thickness in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes.

COMPARISON
Chest x-ray dated 2025-08-30.

FINDINGS
Lungs: There is a 3.2 x 2.8 cm spiculated mass in the posterior segment of
the right upper lobe, with pleural tagging and an associated small focus of
adjacent ground-glass opacity. The mass is highly suspicious for primary
lung malignancy. No additional pulmonary nodules are identified. Background
lung demonstrates centrilobular emphysematous change, predominantly upper-
lobe distributed.

Mediastinum and hila: A 1.4 cm short-axis right hilar lymph node is
present. Subcarinal nodes measure up to 0.9 cm. No pathologically enlarged
left hilar or contralateral mediastinal nodes. No pericardial effusion.

Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

Chest wall and bony thorax: No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions to
suggest metastatic disease. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic
spine.

Upper abdomen (limited): The visualized portions of the liver, spleen,
adrenal glands, and kidneys appear within normal limits. No adrenal
nodules.

IMPRESSION
1. 3.2 cm spiculated right upper lobe mass, highly suspicious for primary
   bronchogenic carcinoma. Tissue diagnosis recommended.
2. Right hilar lymphadenopathy (1.4 cm), suspicious for nodal involvement.
3. Background centrilobular emphysema.

Recommend CT-guided core biopsy and PET-CT for staging.

Electronically signed: K. Yamamoto, MD — Thoracic Radiology — 2025-09-08
